perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010, unless renewed by agreement or sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010, with no option for renewal regardless of agreement unless sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly stating there is 'no option for renewal regardless of agreement' it contradicts the earlier part of the sentence that previously allowed for renewal by agreement. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about the actual term and whether the agreement can be extended, as it contradicts its initial meaning. The contradiction is within Section 1 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010, with no option for renewal regardless of agreement unless sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement, as it removes the possibility of renewal which was initially stated as an option if agreed upon. The original text allowed for renewal by agreement, but the changed text explicitly states that there is 'no option for renewal regardless of agreement'. This directly conflicts with the original agreement and introduces uncertainty. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, meaning that the contradiction is within the Changed Text itself.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if the other party (i) files a petition commencing a voluntary case under the Bankruptcy Code; (ii) makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors; (iii) admits in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due; (iv) seeks, consents to or acquiesces in the appointment of any trustee, receiver or liquidator of it or any part of its property; or (v) has commenced against it an involuntary case under the Bankruptcy Code or a proceeding under any receivership, composition, readjustment, liquidation, insolvency, dissolution or like law or statute, which case or proceeding is not dismissed or vacated within sixty (60) days.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if the other party (i) files a petition commencing a voluntary case under the Bankruptcy Code; (ii) makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors; (iii) admits in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due; (iv) seeks, consents to or acquiesces in the appointment of any trustee, receiver or liquidator of it or any part of its property; or (v) has commenced against it an involuntary case under the Bankruptcy Code or a proceeding under any receivership, composition, readjustment, liquidation, insolvency, dissolution or like law or statute, and no dismissal or vacating is required.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'which case or proceeding is not dismissed or vacated within sixty (60) days' requirement, it now contradicts the original intention of allowing a chance for financial recovery. Now, simply filing such a case is grounds for termination, even if resolved swiftly and successfully. This introduces contradiction on the terms of Termination within section 2.(a) itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination (a) Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if the other party (i) files a petition commencing a voluntary case under the Bankruptcy Code; (ii) makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors; (iii) admits in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due; (iv) seeks, consents to or acquiesces in the appointment of any trustee, receiver or liquidator of it or any part of its property; or (v) has commenced against it an involuntary case under the Bankruptcy Code or a proceeding under any receivership, composition, readjustment, liquidation, insolvency, dissolution or like law or statute, and no dismissal or vacating is required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for dismissal or vacating of bankruptcy proceedings within 60 days, making the mere filing of such a case grounds for immediate termination. This directly contradicts the original intention implied in the unedited clause of allowing a chance for financial recovery. The change creates a situation where a resolved bankruptcy filing (within the original 60 days) would still trigger termination, creating a direct in-text contradiction within the Termination (a) clause.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party may assign its rights or powers under this Agreement without the express written consent of the other, which consent shall • General Liability: Vendor shall maintain commercial general liability (CGL) insurance with a limit of not less than $1 million each occurrence.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may assign its rights or powers under this Agreement without the express written consent of the other, which consent is at the sole discretion of the assignor party. • General Liability: Vendor shall maintain commercial general liability (CGL) insurance with a limit of not less than $1 million each occurrence.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Neither party may assign' to 'Either party may assign', this clause introduces an internal contradiction. The changed clause directly contradicts the original requirement for written consent from the *other* party, giving all power to the assigning party and nullifying consent from the other party. The in-text contradiction here makes the assignment conditions ambiguous and uncertain, specifically in section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment Either party may assign its rights or powers under this Agreement without the express written consent of the other, which consent is at the sole discretion of the assignor party. • General Liability: Vendor shall maintain commercial general liability (CGL) insurance with a limit of not less than $1 million each occurrence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the assignment clause, stating that 'Either party may assign its rights or powers under this Agreement without the express written consent of the other, which consent is at the sole discretion of the assignor party.' This directly contradicts the original intent requiring consent from the *other* party. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text which means the change creates a contradiction in the same text. This creates a structural flaw, as the clause now implies that either party can assign rights without the other's consent, essentially nullifying the requirement for mutual agreement.
